message:
docs: split MultiIndex capability into Row vs Column (br-frankenpandas-0yz7)

Reality-check HIGH 0yz7 flagged that the single "MultiIndex | Yes (foundation)"
row in the capability table conflates two very different states:

- Column MultiIndex DOES have real foundation: DataFrame carries
  column_multiindex: Option<MultiIndex>, br-ch4o wires grouped-
  window output through it, stack/unstack scaffolds target it.

- Row MultiIndex does NOT exist on DataFrame — DataFrame::index is
  still Index<IndexLabel>, not MultiIndex<Vec<IndexLabel>>. br-r4au's
  attempt to emit real tuple keys from grouped rolling/resample was
  reverted to flat Utf8 keys ("a|1" via a separator) in br-ch4o.
  .loc[("a", 1)] tuple-key indexing, groupby([k1, k2]).sum tuple
  output, and the set_index_multi ↔ reset_index round-trip are all
  scaffolded but not behaviorally complete.

Two surgical README edits to stop conflating them:

1. Capability table (README.md:33): the single "MultiIndex" row
   becomes two rows —
     Column MultiIndex | Yes (foundation)
     Row MultiIndex    | Scaffolded (standalone type; DataFrame
                         row-axis integration pending — see
                         Limitations)
   The "(foundation)" qualifier now only attaches to the state that
   actually has one; Row MultiIndex gets the honest "Scaffolded"
   label that matches the in-tree reality.

2. MultiIndex section (README.md:396): the sentence that said
   "set_index_multi ... creates composite index labels" is upgraded
   to name the actual mechanism — "flattens the composite keys into
   a single Index<IndexLabel> using a separator (e.g. 'a|1')" —
   and enumerates the specific surfaces still scaffolded (tuple .loc,
   real row-MultiIndex groupby output, stack/unstack, reset_index
   round-trips). Cross-references the new capability row and the
   existing Limitations entry so future readers land on the same
   reality from all three entry points.

No change to the existing Limitations row (README.md:1372) — it was
already honest about the integration gap; the edits above just make
the hero table and the inline prose line up with it instead of
contradicting it.

Follow-up epic filed: br-frankenpandas-1zzp
([EPIC] Row MultiIndex — full DataFrame row-axis integration) —
6-slice umbrella covering the DataFrame row-axis type, GroupBy
multi-key output, tuple .loc indexing, reshape round-trips, IO
multi-row header/index block, and live-oracle packet coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -30,7 +30,8 @@
 | Index alignment semantics | Yes | No (no index) | Yes (AACE) |
 | GroupBy with named aggregation | Yes | Yes (different syntax) | Yes (`agg_named`) |
 | `eval()`/`query()` string expressions | Yes | No | Yes |
-| MultiIndex | Yes | No | Yes (foundation) |
+| Column MultiIndex | Yes | No | Yes (foundation) |
+| Row MultiIndex | Yes | No | Scaffolded (standalone type; DataFrame row-axis integration pending — see Limitations) |
 | Categorical dtype | Yes | Yes | Yes (metadata layer) |
 | 7 IO formats (CSV/JSON/JSONL/Parquet/Excel/SQL/Feather) | Yes | Partial | Yes |
 | Conformance testing against pandas oracle | - | - | Yes (20+ packet suites) |
@@ -393,7 +394,7 @@ MultiIndex {
 
 Constructors mirror pandas: `from_tuples`, `from_arrays`, `from_product` (Cartesian product). Operations: `get_level_values(level)`, `droplevel(level)` → `MultiIndexOrIndex`, `swaplevel(i,j)`, `reorder_levels(order)`, `to_flat_index(sep)`.
 
-DataFrame integration via `set_index_multi(&["col1", "col2"], drop, sep)` creates composite index labels, and `to_multi_index(&["col1", "col2"])` extracts a standalone MultiIndex from columns.
+DataFrame integration via `set_index_multi(&["col1", "col2"], drop, sep)` currently flattens the composite keys into a single `Index<IndexLabel>` using a separator (e.g. `"a|1"`); `to_multi_index(&["col1", "col2"])` extracts a standalone tuple-keyed `MultiIndex` from columns. Tuple-keyed `.loc[("a", 1)]` indexing and real row-MultiIndex output from `groupby([k1, k2]).sum` / `stack` / `unstack` / `reset_index` round-trips are scaffolded but not behaviorally complete — tracked under the Row MultiIndex row in the capability table and the Limitations section.
 
 ### String Accessor
